message:
chore: close completed beads after truthfulness and resilience work

Five issues closed:
- flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.5.2: catalog truth for host-backed
  template/validate/example materialization
- flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.7.2: workflow truth for intent and
  planning layers
- flywheel_connectors-qnchs.14: error recovery and resilience patterns
- flywheel_connectors-qnchs.14.2: auto-retry with exponential backoff
  and jitter
- flywheel_connectors-qnchs.14.3: fallback connector routing and
  circuit-breaker patterns

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

4. Unit, integration, and transcript verification responsibilities are explicit across the child beads.","notes":"Implementation lanes: `1g7z0.29.5.1` through `.5.3`. Closure overlay: `1g7z0.29.10`. This is the host-authoritative catalog and tool-surface track.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:07.426154Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","updated_at":"2026-03-11T21:31:44.794301Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["catalog","fwc","mcp","truthfulness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:07.426154Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1530,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.5","author":"QuietBasin","text":"## Track rationale: catalog surfaces must reflect the running system

This track translates the plan requirement that host introspection become authoritative for discovery/search/template/validate/export-tools/serve-mcp. The high-level rule is: in live mode, anything that claims to describe or expose the runtime inventory must derive that answer from host-authoritative inventory and introspection.

### Decomposition
- `29.5.1` covers host-backed discovery, search, suggest, list, show, ops, schema, and examples.
- `29.5.2` covers template, validate, and example generation from live introspection.
- `29.5.3` covers export-tools and serve-mcp over live inventory.

### Why this track is separate from the generic runtime-boundary track
The runtime boundary decides which source of truth is allowed. This catalog track makes the high-frequency exploration and tool-exposure surfaces actually obey that boundary. It is the difference between a theoretically honest system and one whose day-to-day discovery UX is genuinely trustworthy.

### Future-self cautions
- Workspace inspection is still valuable, but only when explicitly labeled as offline or artifact-oriented.
- Tool export and MCP serving are especially sensitive because they amplify mistakes into downstream agent ecosystems. If those surfaces are wrong, every client built on top of them becomes wrong too.","created_at":"2026-03-11T18:47:20Z"},{"id":1559,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.5","author":"QuietBasin","text":"Planning clarification: `1g7z0.29.5.1` now owns discovery/browse surfaces and availability metadata, while `1g7z0.29.5.2` owns template/example materialization plus validation payloads.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.6","title":"[FWC] Truthful metadata contract: unknown stays unknown and nothing gets invented","description":"Remove any invented runtime metadata from host and CLI surfaces.

If archetype, rate limits, event support, auth requirements, or related capability descriptors are not known truthfully, the system must say so instead of manufacturing a plausible default. The goal is to make incorrect confidence impossible.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The host/CLI model forbids invented runtime metadata and replaces it with explicit known/unknown/unsupported vocabulary.
2. User-facing output becomes more trustworthy even when less information is available.
3. The resulting state model is detailed enough for discovery, config, simulate, alias/completion, and workflow layers to consume consistently.
4. Verification obligations for both local rendering and host-backed behavior are explicit.","notes":"This is the semantic hygiene lane. It exists because misleading metadata is one of the easiest ways to make a control plane feel functional while quietly lying to users. The implementation should prefer absence, provenance, and explicit uncertainty over convenience defaults.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:07.894799Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","updated_at":"2026-03-11T18:47:40.105870Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","metadata","truthfulness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:27:07.894799Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1531,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.6","author":"QuietBasin","text":"## Track rationale: unknown must stay unknown

This track is the explicit response to the plan requirement to remove synthetic defaults. The issue is not only incorrect data; it is the false confidence created when the system substitutes plausible values for missing truth.

### Decomposition
- `29.6.1` removes invented archetypes, rate limits, capability flags, and similar synthetic metadata defaults.
- `29.6.2` defines the explicit host/CLI vocabulary for unknown, unsupported, unavailable, and not-yet-measured states.

### Design intent
The end state should make it mechanically harder to lie accidentally. If the runtime does not know something, the type shape, JSON envelope, and user-facing copy should all bias toward explicit uncertainty rather than convenience defaults.

### Things future implementers should preserve
- Unknown, unsupported, unavailable, and not-yet-measured are not synonyms.
- Provenance matters: declared-by-connector, observed-by-host, measured-at-runtime, and inferred-from-policy should not blur together.
- Good UX here is not about hiding uncertainty; it is about making uncertainty legible and actionable.","created_at":"2026-03-11T18:47:40Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.7","title":"[FWC] Truthful workflow execution: no planning-only runtime verbs in the canonical control path","description":"Clean up the workflow and intent layers so the canonical agent path never implies that planning-only or stubbed runtime control exists when it does not.

### Decomposition
- `29.7.1` makes `pipe`, `recipe run`, and `pipeline run` either host-backed or explicit refusals.
- `29.7.2` constrains intent/planning layers so they only expose host-backed control primitives.
- `29.7.3` standardizes the output semantics for unsupported, planned, offline-only, unavailable, denied, and live-runtime states.

### Key idea to preserve
The branch is not anti-workflow. It is anti-ambiguity. High-level workflow affordances should remain ambitious, but they must carry truthful receipts, logs, and refusal semantics so an agent never confuses a plan artifact with a completed side effect.","created_at":"2026-03-11T18:47:40Z"}]}

This track exists so the branch does not stop at semantic design. A truthful runtime boundary is only real if regressions are cheap to detect and operator guidance is explicit enough that future sessions do not have to reconstruct the original plan.

### Decomposition
- `29.8.1` host integration matrix for live/offline boundaries, auth, simulate, metadata, and MCP truth.
- `29.8.2` end-to-end scenarios and regression gates for the no-fakes control-plane invariants.
- `29.8.3` docs, playbooks, and migration guidance for the host-first truthful model.
- `29.8.4` fast unit and snapshot invariants for local semantic truthfulness.
- `29.8.5` transcript scripts, detailed logging, and replay artifact contracts.

### Important future-self reminders
- Verification here is multi-layer by design: local semantic invariants, host-backed integration, transcript-style E2E evidence, and docs/playbooks.
- The branch is not done when code works once on one machine. It is done when future engineers can see exactly what invariant failed and what evidence proves the intended semantics.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.14.3","title":"[CUAL-N.3] Fallback connector routing and circuit-breaker patterns","description":"# Fallback Connector Routing and Circuit-Breaker Patterns

## Goal
When a connector fails persistently, automatically protect against cascading failures via circuit breakers and suggest alternative connectors for the same operation.

## Deliverables

### Circuit Breaker State Machine
- States: CLOSED (normal) -> OPEN (failing, reject immediately) -> HALF-OPEN (probe)
- Transition: CLOSED->OPEN at 50% error rate over 10-request window
- OPEN->HALF-OPEN after 30s cooldown
- HALF-OPEN->CLOSED on 1 success, HALF-OPEN->OPEN on 1 failure
- State persisted in ~/.fwc/circuit_breakers.json per connector
- When OPEN: immediate FCP_ERR_CIRCUIT_OPEN with time until next probe

### Fallback Suggestions
- When circuit is OPEN, suggest alternative connectors for the same operation
- Uses B.3 (routing) capability families and connector categories
- Ranked by: health status, zone availability, capability match

### CLI Integration
- `fwc invoke` checks circuit state before executing; rejects if OPEN
- `fwc health` shows circuit breaker states alongside health status
- `fwc circuit reset <connector>` force-resets circuit to CLOSED
- `fwc circuit status` shows all circuit states

### Output Format
- Default: TOON showing circuit state, error counts, cooldown timer, fallback suggestions
- `--json` flag for machine-readable circuit state and fallback list

## Test Criteria
Unit tests (10 minimum):
- CLOSED -> OPEN transition at error threshold
- OPEN -> HALF_OPEN after cooldown timer
- HALF_OPEN -> CLOSED on success
- HALF_OPEN -> OPEN on failure
- Persistent state across fwc invocations (file-backed)
- Concurrent request handling in HALF_OPEN (single probe, queue others)
- Cooldown timer accuracy (not early/late)
- Force-reset command returns to CLOSED
- Fallback suggestions from B.3 routing match operation capability
- Circuit status output in TOON and JSON formats

## Acceptance Criteria
- Circuit state is correct after any sequence of success/failure events
- State survives process restart (file-backed)
- FCP_ERR_CIRCUIT_OPEN includes time-until-probe and fallback list
- Force-reset is immediate and atomic
- TOON output is human-scannable, --json output is machine-parseable","acceptance_criteria":"1. Fallback routing and circuit-breaker behavior are explicit about confidence, eligibility, and the difference between recommended alternatives and automatic failover.
2. Breakers fail safe, preserve operator trust, and avoid fighting with retry or throttling controllers.
3. Unit, integration, and transcript-style E2E verification with detailed logging are required for failover, breaker-open, breaker-reset, and no-safe-fallback cases.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:35:49.101017Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-11T10:08:46.366181Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["circuit-breaker","cual","errors","fallback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.14.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:36:00.568711Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.14.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:35:49.101017Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.14.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.14.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:36:00.011117Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1336,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.14.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"DESIGN CONSIDERATION: Circuit breaker state machine must be per-connector and persistent across fwc invocations (not just in-memory). OPEN->HALF_OPEN after 30s cooldown. HALF_OPEN->CLOSED on 1 success, HALF_OPEN->OPEN on 1 failure. TESTING: ≥10 unit tests: full state machine transitions, persistent state across invocations, concurrent request handling in HALF_OPEN, cooldown timer accuracy, force-reset command, fallback suggestions from B.3 routing.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:25:18Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.15","title":"[CUAL-T] Comprehensive Testing & Quality Assurance","description":"# Track T: Comprehensive Testing and Quality Assurance

## Why This Track Exists
The CUAL implementation has ~890 unit tests across individual modules, but there are critical testing gaps:
1. NO E2E test scripts exercising full workflows (discover -> search -> validate -> invoke -> verify)
2. NO integration tests validating cross-module interactions (search + validate + pipe + history)
3. NO performance benchmarks for search indexing, batch throughput, or MCP server latency
4. Dead-code modules have unit tests but no integration with CLI dispatch

## What This Track Delivers
- E2E bash test scripts with detailed logging for every CUAL track
- Rust integration test suite (tests/cual_integration.rs) for cross-module flows
- Performance benchmark suite
- Test observability: structured logging in tests so failures are debuggable
- Regression test gates that run before any CUAL release

## Testing Philosophy
- Every CUAL feature must have at least one happy-path E2E script
- Every error path must have a unit test with the exact error code verified
- Integration tests must exercise the full CLI dispatch path, not just module internals
- E2E scripts must emit structured logging (JSON lines) for automated verification","acceptance_criteria":"1. The CUAL test track covers comprehensive unit, integration, transcript-style E2E, and benchmark verification across all implemented command families with detailed redaction-safe logging.
2. Dispatch tests exercise the real CLI entrypoints and structured error envelopes, not only module internals, and every major track produces replayable artifacts for regression triage.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.15.1","title":"[CUAL-T.1] E2E bash test scripts for all CUAL tracks with structured logging","description":"## Goal
Create comprehensive E2E bash test scripts in scripts/e2e/ that exercise full CUAL workflows end-to-end via the fwc CLI binary.

## Scripts to Create (one per major capability)

### Track A: MCP/Tool Schema
- cual_export_tools.sh: fwc export-tools --format mcp, verify JSON schema structure, check tool count matches connector count, verify ai_hints preserved
- cual_serve_mcp.sh: start fwc serve-mcp in background, send JSON-RPC initialize, tools/list, tools/call, verify responses, clean shutdown

### Track B: Discovery
- cual_search.sh: fwc search 'send message' --json, verify results include slack/discord/telegram, verify scoring order, test faceted filters (--capability, --risk), test empty results

### Track C: Auth
- cual_auth_lifecycle.sh: fwc auth add mock-connector --token xxx, fwc auth list (verify redacted), fwc auth test mock-connector (with mock server), fwc auth remove, verify gone

### Track D: History
- cual_history_replay.sh: fwc invoke (mock), fwc history --json (verify entry), fwc replay <id> --dry-run, fwc diff --last-two

### Track E: Output
- cual_output_formats.sh: fwc invoke ... --extract '.title', verify jq output; --format table, verify columns; --format csv; --template '{{title}}'

### Track F: Pipeline
- cual_pipeline.sh: fwc pipe A B --map ..., verify output; fwc pipeline validate my-pipeline.toml; fwc pipeline dry-run

### Track G: Bulk
- cual_batch_map.sh: fwc map op --inputs '[...]' --concurrency 2, verify NDJSON output, verify progress; fwc batch ops.jsonl

### Track H: Streaming
- cual_events.sh: fwc events tail (with mock), verify output format, test --type filter, test --match filter

### Track I: Rate Limits
- cual_rate_limits.sh: fwc rate-limits --json, verify pool status format

### Track J: Health
- cual_health.sh: fwc health --json, verify status fields; fwc doctor mock-connector

### Track K: Schema
- cual_schema_validate.sh: fwc schema op --required-only, verify output; fwc template op, verify JSON template; fwc validate op --input bad.json, verify error format

### Track L/M/N: (when unblocked)
- cual_zones.sh, cual_session.sh, cual_error_recovery.sh

## Logging Standard
Every script MUST:
- Set -euo pipefail
- Log to stdout with: [TIMESTAMP] [PASS/FAIL] test_name: description
- Use JSON structured output for verification (jq assertions)
- Clean up temp files and background processes on exit (trap)
- Exit 0 on all pass, exit 1 on any fail
- Support --verbose flag for detailed output

## Location
scripts/e2e/cual_*.sh (follows existing pattern)

## Acceptance Criteria
- scripts/e2e/run_matrix.sh includes all CUAL scripts
- All scripts pass with mock connectors (no real API calls)
- Each script has at least 5 distinct assertions
- Every error path tested produces the expected FCP_ERR_* code","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E bash or shell scripts cover representative end-to-end flows for all implemented CUAL tracks using the real CLI entrypoints.
2. Scripts emit detailed structured logs, timestamps, exit codes, and replay artifacts that make failures directly diagnosable.

- `cual_mesh.sh` covering `fwc mesh status/nodes/use/target/explain/availability` plus mesh-aware lifecycle/install commands using `--node`, `--cohort`, and active mesh context

Helper scripts may exist if the matrix grows, but `run_matrix.sh` and bead references should preserve these canonical names.

- emit `scenario_id`, `step_id`, timestamp, command-under-test, and pass/fail state for every assertion block
- persist structured stdout/stderr captures plus any receipt handle, resume token, rollout id, or artifact path needed for replay
- include redaction assertions so secrets and sensitive handles never leak into logs
- print a deterministic replay hint whenever a scenario produces a durable handle or a recoverable  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.15.2","title":"[CUAL-T.2] Rust integration test suite for cross-module CUAL workflows","description":"## Goal
Create a Rust integration test file that exercises cross-module CUAL workflows: search -> validate -> invoke -> history -> replay.

## Test File
crates/fwc/tests/cual_integration.rs (or inline in relevant modules)

## Integration Test Scenarios

### Workflow 1: Discovery-to-Invoke
1. Build search index from mock connector introspections
2. Search for 'create issue', verify correct operation found
3. Generate template for found operation
4. Validate a well-formed input against schema
5. Validate a malformed input, verify structured error
6. (When invoke is wired) Execute operation, verify history entry created

### Workflow 2: Batch-with-Throttle
1. Configure throttle with mock rate limit pools
2. Create batch input (10 operations)
3. Execute with concurrency=2
4. Verify throttle delays are applied when quota is low
5. Verify progress tracking reports correct counts
6. Verify all results collected even with partial failures

### Workflow 3: Pipe-with-Validation
1. Set up two mock operations with known schemas
2. Create pipe mapping from A output to B input
3. Validate pipe mapping against both schemas
4. Execute pipe in dry-run mode
5. Verify estimated API calls match expected

### Workflow 4: MCP Server Protocol
1. Initialize McpServerState with mock connectors
2. Send tools/list request, verify tool schemas match introspection
3. Send tools/call, verify routing to correct connector
4. Send resources/list, verify connector state resources
5. Send invalid request, verify JSON-RPC error response

### Workflow 5: Auth-History-Replay Lifecycle
1. Add credential to store
2. Execute operation (verify credential used)
3. Query history for the operation
4. Replay operation from history
5. Verify replay used same credential

### Workflow 6: Event-Filter-React
1. Create mock event stream
2. Subscribe with type filter
3. Verify only matching events pass through
4. Add reactive rule
5. Verify rule triggers on matching events
6. Verify throttle prevents excess triggers

## Test Infrastructure
- Use wiremock::MockServer for HTTP mocking
- Use tokio::test for async tests
- Use tempdir for credential store and history files
- Each test is fully isolated (no shared state)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 6 workflows have passing tests
- Tests run in < 10 seconds total
- Each test has clear setup/act/assert sections with comments
- Tests are deterministic (no flaky behavior)","acceptance_criteria":"1. Integration tests exercise cross-module CUAL workflows through realistic dispatch and shared state boundaries rather than isolated unit seams.
2. Coverage includes success, ambiguity, denial, recovery, and partial-failure interactions across search, auth, history, output, pipeline, and event surfaces as implemented.
3. Detailed logs and reproducible fixtures make cross-module regressions attributable quickly.","notes":"Extend the integration suite with explicit O/P/Q workflows:
